seoTitle: Dongolocking vs Donnelly River — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Dongolocking and Donnelly River. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Dongolocking vs Donnelly River: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dongolocking and Donnelly River.
heading: Dongolocking vs Donnelly River — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dongolocking, Western Australia and Donnelly River, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Dongolocking has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Donnelly River (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dongolocking or Donnelly River safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dongolocking has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Donnelly River scores 29/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dongolocking scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Donnelly River scores 60/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dongolocking or Donnelly River?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Dongolocking has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
